title: amauropelta ruiziana
intro: Amauropelta ruiziana, also known as the Chilean fern, is a species of fern native to the Andes Mountains in South America, specifically in Chile and Argentina. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Amauropelta ruiziana include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparation of this plant is typically made by drying and infusing the leaves in hot water, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application for skin irritations.
